The two cardinal trust primitives are proven generatively at the cheap layer-2 boundary (debt-never-accumulates posture).

Phase 03 (indexer_ingest — US-AV-001) ships AV-1..AV-7 over the REAL openlore-indexer binary + FakeIngestSource + the fixture real-z6Mk PLC resolver + the REAL index.duckdb. AV-1 is the slice-05 WALKING-SKELETON beat 1 (the index exists, is trustworthy, is searchable). AV-4 is the ADR-026 gold path (the REAL z6Mk PLC decode runs with the env seam UNSET; the did_unresolvable reject path per ADR-026 surfaces via AV-3/AV-6). AV-5 is the capability boundary (signing-incapable + touches no local store, ADR-023). AV-6 is the wire->probe->use startup refusal. AV-7 is public-data-only ingest.

AV-10 (public-data banner), AV-11 (universal [verified]), AV-14 (B1 localhost transport) are the other Release-1 cardinal gates; AV-23 (--show trust surface) chains off the AV-8 result's cid.

Phase 05 (dimensions + funnel + share + counter — US-AV-003/005/006 + the Release-1 sad paths, Release 2/3) ships AV-12/15/16/17/18/19/20/21/22/24/25/26/27/28/29. Three cardinal release gates are EXPLICIT load-bearing single-scenario steps, never merged: AV-3 (verify-before-index; 03-03), AV-9 (anti-merging; 04-02), AV-13 (local-first; 04-05). The two cheap generative halves (AVC-1 verify-gate; AVC-2 anti-merging) are also single steps (02-01 + 02-04). Per DISTILL §7 every gate has >=1 layer-3 behavioral assertion AND the two cardinal trust guarantees ALSO have a layer-2 generative property.

Anti-merging at NETWORK SCALE (WD-103/WD-120/I-AV-2; the direct descendant of slice-03 I-FED-1 + slice-04 I-GRAPH-1/2) is THREE semantically orthogonal layers: TYPE (non-Option author_did on IndexedClaim/NetworkResultRow + a per-author NetworkSearchResult with NO merged-row API — Phase 01 lands the types; AVC-4/AVC-5 prove it at layer 2); STRUCTURAL (xtask no_cross_table_join_elides_author EXTENDED to the adapter-index-store SQL — Phase 01 + Phase 03); BEHAVIORAL (network_result_preserves_attribution AV-9 + AVC-2/AV-2/AV-16/AV-27 — Phases 02/03/04/05). Aggregation (distinct_author_count) happens in the PURE appview-domain core (Rust), NEVER in SQL (no GROUP BY across authors); the index-store queries return per-claim attributed rows. There is NO consensus/merged/aggregate table — the load-bearing ABSENCE (AV-2 asserts it).

Verified-before-index (WD-104/WD-121/I-AV-1) REUSES the pure claim-domain verify + compute_cid core at ingest (no second verification path); only the Index arm reaches the store; verified_against NOT NULL + CHECK <> '' is a schema invariant; the [verified] marker is a CONSTRUCTION guarantee (there is no verified BOOLEAN column, no [unverified] state). The production pubkey decode is REAL (ADR-026): claim_domain::decode_ed25519_multibase (pure) + the verify-only IdentityResolvePort PLC resolution (effect); the slice-03 OPENLORE_PEER_PUBKEY_HEX_<did> env seam is RETAINED but release-forbidden (xtask no_pubkey_seam_in_release_build; AV-4 runs the REAL decode with the seam UNSET).

The indexer is signing-INCAPABLE + holds NO local store (ADR-023/I-AV-5; mirrors slice-02 I-SCR-1): three layers — TYPE (verify-only IdentityResolvePort + read-only IngestSourcePort; no sign/publish method exists), STRUCTURAL (xtask indexer_holds_no_signing_or_local_store + the CLI-links-no-HTTP-server clause replacing the narrowed deny.toml axum ban), BEHAVIORAL (the composition-root capability_boundary_probe + AV-5). The two composition roots are DISJOINT (I-3 extended to both binaries): cli wires the USER's adapters + the index-query CLIENT; openlore-indexer wires the indexer's adapters; neither wires the other's.

Earned Trust (the four NEW adapters' probe() bodies) is DELIVER's adapter-integration deliverable per DESIGN §6.3, landed in Phase 01 as stubs + filled in Phase 03/04 against the substrate lies: adapter-index-store fsync-honored-on-container-substrate (overlayfs/DrvFs/tmpfs no-op -> refuse storage.fsync_unhonored) + attribution round-trip + no-merge-schema; adapter-atproto-ingest source-reachability + the network-lies check (a fixture tampered/cid-mismatch record REJECTED before the index); adapter-atproto-did the REAL z6Mk decode verifies a known-good sig + rejects a tampered one (the gold path); adapter-xrpc-query-server bind+serve + the response carries per-result author_did; adapter-index-query reachable-shape + the INVERTED/degradation check (unreachable -> soft Unreachable, NOT a startup refusal). xtask check-probes enforces non-stub probe bodies. AV-6 is the only probe concern surfaced as an ACCEPTANCE scenario (the user-visible startup REFUSAL the probes drive); the rest are below the driving-port boundary (out of DISTILL scope per DD-AV-7).

steps.name: AVC-1 verify-before-index gate indexes iff verified and cid matches (PROPERTY; KPI-AV-3 generative half)
criteria: ingest_decision returns Index(_) IF AND ONLY IF claim_domain::verify(record.raw_payload, key) == Ok AND claim_domain::compute_cid(record.raw_payload) == record.published_cid
on Index: claim.author_did == record.raw_payload.author AND claim.verified_against != ""; on Reject: NO IndexedClaim is produced
any adversarial record (unsigned / tampered-signature / cid-mismatch) returns Reject(reason); reuses the SAME pure claim_domain::verify + compute_cid (no second verification path)
Property over arbitrary_raw_records() (~50% valid, ~50% adversarial split across unsigned/tampered/cid-mismatch) paired with the resolved VerificationKey
AVC-1 passes
steps.implementation_notes: Per data-models.md §The verified-before-index invariant + ADR-026 + WD-104/WD-121 + I-AV-1 + KPI-AV-3 (the cheap generative half) + DESIGN 5.1 inv 2. CARDINAL gate: this IS the verify-before-index disprover proven generatively at the cheap layer-2 boundary. A mutant that lets a tampered record through, or fabricates a verified_against on a rejected record, fails LOUDLY here (D-D40 mutates ingest.rs at >=95%). LOAD-BEARING no-second-path: ingest_decision calls claim_domain::verify + compute_cid (the SAME pure core the indexer runs at ingest AND the CLI renders as [verified]). The decode the verify consumes is the ADR-026 real path (the exhaustive decode-boundary + decode-encode-identity unit/mutation tests are DELIVER's claim-domain inner loop, out of DISTILL scope per DD-AV-7). TEST PARADIGM (mandatory): this is LAYER-2 PROPTEST per Mandate 9 — proptest IS the implementation strategy; generator arbitrary_raw_records() over the small bounded universe (valid + the three adversarial arms); assert the iff + author-derivation + non-empty verified_against on Index + no-claim on Reject. The exhaustive per-RejectReason-arm coverage lives in crates/appview-domain/src/#[cfg(test)] (DELIVER inner loop, DD-AV-7). State-delta: universe = the IngestOutcome discriminant + (on Index) claim.author_did/verified_against/cid; unchanged() covers the rest. Acceptance scenario is the @property gate. STAGE CONTEXT: Stage 0 NEW code.

steps.name: AVC-3a ingest_decision is deterministic (PROPERTY)
criteria: ingest_decision(record, key) == ingest_decision(record, key) for all inputs — byte-identical IngestOutcome (same discriminant; same IndexedClaim or same RejectReason)
no clock read / hash-map iteration-order leak makes the gate non-deterministic (the [verified] construction guarantee precondition)
Property over arbitrary (record, key)
AVC-3a passes
steps.implementation_notes: Per DESIGN 5.1 inv 4 + I-AV-1. Determinism is the reproducibility precondition for the verify gate; it is structural in a pure core (no clock, no I/O) but a clock read or hash-map-order leak would break it — the test pins it. AVC-3 splits into AVC-3a (ingest, this step) + AVC-3b (compose, 02-05); appview_core.rs has 9 distinct #[test] fns for the 8 numbered scenarios. Mirrors slice-04 SC-2 (scoring determinism) + slice-02 scraper-domain determinism. TEST PARADIGM (mandatory): LAYER-2 PROPTEST per Mandate 9; assert ingest_decision(r,k) == ingest_decision(r,k) over arbitrary_raw_records(); universe = the full IngestOutcome value; unchanged() N/A (full-value equality). Single-example FALLBACK only with // bypass:. STAGE CONTEXT: Stage 0 NEW code.

steps.name: AVC-4 indexed author is derived from the signed payload (PROPERTY; anti-merging type/derivation)
criteria: for every record that ingest_decision indexes, the resulting IndexedClaim.author_did equals record.raw_payload.author byte-for-byte
attribution comes from the SIGNED bytes (the signature covers them), NEVER from the unsigned forgeable provenance (source_pds)
an author can never be substituted or dropped between the signed payload and the indexed row
Property over arbitrary records that verify
AVC-4 passes
steps.implementation_notes: Per data-models.md §Invariant (the row's attribution is derived from the signed payload) + I-AV-2 + WD-103 (anti-merging type/derivation half). This is the type/derivation half of anti-merging at ingest: the indexed author is the signed payload's author, never the unsigned source_pds — a forgeable field. Complements AVC-1 (the gate) and AVC-5 (the no-merged-row type contract). TEST PARADIGM (mandatory): LAYER-2 PROPTEST per Mandate 9; forall record indexed by ingest_decision: IndexedClaim.author_did == record.raw_payload.author (byte-equal); universe = claim.author_did vs record.raw_payload.author (port-exposed boundary values). STAGE CONTEXT: Stage 0 NEW code.

steps.name: AVC-2 compose_results preserves every author (PROPERTY; KPI-AV-2 generative half)
criteria: for an arbitrary non-empty Vec<IndexedClaim>: distinct_author_count == COUNT(DISTINCT author_did) over the input (a COUNT over attributed rows, NEVER a merged aggregate)
total_claims == rows.len() (no claim dropped); the flattened by_author rows == the input rows as a MULTISET (every (author_did, cid) appears EXACTLY once in exactly one author group)
two input rows with identical (subject, object) but distinct author_did land in DIFFERENT author groups (never collapsed); there is NO NetworkSearchResult API exposing a merged multi-author row
Property over a small bounded universe (3 subjects x 2 objects x 3 authors, confidence in [0,1], every verified_against non-empty)
AVC-2 passes
steps.implementation_notes: Per data-models.md §The anti-merging-at-network-scale invariant + WD-103/WD-120 + I-AV-2 + KPI-AV-2 (the cheap generative half) + DESIGN §10. CARDINAL gate: this IS the anti-merging-at-network-scale disprover proven generatively. The network-scale descendant of slice-03 I-FED-1 + slice-04 I-GRAPH-1/2. A mutant that GROUP-BYs object/subject (dropping author_did into a faceless consensus count) fails LOUDLY here (D-D40 mutates compose.rs at >=95%). There is no merged-row API to assert against — the absence is the type-level guarantee (AVC-5). TEST PARADIGM (mandatory): LAYER-2 PROPTEST per Mandate 9; generator arbitrary_indexed_claims() over the small bounded universe (so generated sets exercise single-author, multi-author, and identical-content-distinct-author pairings); assert distinct_author_count == COUNT(DISTINCT author_did), total_claims == rows.len(), flattened by_author == input rows as a multiset, identical-content-distinct-author rows in different groups. State-delta: universe = result.distinct_author_count, result.total_claims, the multiset of (author_did,cid) flattened from by_author; NEVER an internal grouping-map field. STAGE CONTEXT: Stage 0 NEW code.

steps.name: AVC-3b compose_results is deterministic (PROPERTY; the share query-not-snapshot precondition)
criteria: compose_results(rows, dimension) == compose_results(rows, dimension) for all inputs — byte-identical NetworkSearchResult (same by_author ordering, same counts, same suggestion)
by_author is sorted by a stable key (no HashMap-iteration-order leak)
Property over arbitrary (rows, dimension)
AVC-3b passes
steps.implementation_notes: Per DESIGN 5.1 inv 4 + I-AV-8 + US-AV-006. Determinism underpins the --share 're-running the query yields the same result' contract (the link encodes the QUERY, not a snapshot — AV-26/AV-27/AV-28) AND the wire-shape the B1 transport relies on. AVC-3b is the compose half of the AVC-3 determinism split (AVC-3a is the ingest half, 02-02). A HashMap-ordered by_author would break it — pin the stable tiebreak (data-models.md §NetworkSearchResult). TEST PARADIGM (mandatory): LAYER-2 PROPTEST per Mandate 9; assert compose_results(rows,dim) == compose_results(rows,dim) over arbitrary_indexed_claims(); the determinism is enforced by sorting by_author with a stable key, not insertion order; universe = the full NetworkSearchResult value. STAGE CONTEXT: Stage 0 NEW code.

steps.name: AVC-5 two identical-content distinct-author claims compose to two groups (example; Gate type-level)
criteria: compose over the canonical US-AV-002 Example 2 deno/dependency-pinning pairing (Priya@0.70, Sven@0.65) yields by_author with TWO entries (did:plc:priya-test, did:plc:sven-test), each a single-row group
distinct_author_count == 2; total_claims == 2
the NetworkSearchResult TYPE has no merged-author row to produce (the per-author structure is the only shape) — the load-bearing absence (WD-103)
AVC-5 passes
steps.implementation_notes: Per WD-103/WD-120 + DESIGN §10 (Gate type-level) + I-AV-2 + data-models.md §anti-merging-at-network-scale (deno). Example-pinned (the canonical pairing), NOT a property, per acceptance-tests.md CM-F. This is the type-level half of the three-layer anti-merging enforcement: the aggregate can ALWAYS enumerate its individually-attributed claims — there is NO struct field or method representing the two combined; the absence IS the design. The structural xtask SQL-string layer (01-03) + the behavioral layer-3 render (AV-9, 04-02) complete the three layers. TEST PARADIGM (mandatory): AVC-5 is example-pinned per slice-05 DD-AV (worked pairing); the exhaustive coverage is in crates/appview-domain/src/#[cfg(test)] (DELIVER inner loop). Universe = result.by_author author-set {priya, sven}, distinct_author_count (2), total_claims (2). STAGE CONTEXT: Stage 0 NEW code.

steps.name: AVC-7 every composed row carries a non-empty verified_against (example; universal verified marker)
criteria: a NetworkSearchResult built only from IndexedClaims that came out of ingest_decision's Index arm has EVERY NetworkResultRow with verified_against != ""
there is no verified BOOLEAN; verified_against records that every row WAS verified (the construction guarantee, DESIGN 5.2 #3); the renderer reads it as a universal [verified] marker
there is no mixed-trust list — no [unverified] state to render (US-AV-004 Ex 3)
AVC-7 passes
steps.implementation_notes: Per US-AV-004 boundary + I-AV-1 + data-models.md §verified marker. Example-pinned. Because ingest_decision only ever produces an IndexedClaim on the Index arm (which always sets a non-empty verified_against), every row that reaches compose_results carries the verified marker by construction — the layer-2 proof behind the AV-11 universal-marker release gate (04-04). TEST PARADIGM (mandatory): example-pinned; build a NetworkSearchResult from Index-arm IndexedClaims only; assert every NetworkResultRow.verified_against != ""; universe = { row.verified_against for row in flatten(by_author) }, assert none empty. STAGE CONTEXT: Stage 0 NEW code.

steps.name: AVC-6 countered claim still appears with annotation (example; OD-AV-7 shown-not-applied)
criteria: a claim C referenced by a later indexed claim K with ref_type=counters (both indexed) is STILL present in compose_results' output (total_claims unchanged, C present)
C's row carries counter_annotation == Some(CounterRef { by: K.author_did, cid: K.cid })
the counter is an ANNOTATION, never a filter/removal/down-weight (the load-bearing OD-AV-7 default; mirrors slice-04 WD-85)
AVC-6 passes
steps.implementation_notes: Per OD-AV-7 + WD-119 + I-AV-9 + component-boundaries.md §appview-domain probe responsibilities (counter unit). Example-pinned. annotate_counter_relationship adds an annotation, NEVER removes a row; a code path that filtered or down-weighted C fails here. This is the pure-core behavior the layer-3 render AV-25 (05-11) pins user-visibly. D-D40 mutates annotate_counter_relationship. TEST PARADIGM (mandatory): example-pinned; given claim C countered by indexed claim K, compose_results returns C present with counter_annotation Some(CounterRef{by: K.author, cid: K.cid}); C NOT removed/filtered/down-weighted; universe = presence of C in flatten(by_author), C.counter_annotation (Some), result.total_claims (unchanged by the counter). STAGE CONTEXT: Stage 0 NEW code.

steps.name: AVC-8 near_match_suggestion finds the closest known object (example; empty-result suggestion)
criteria: near_match_suggestion(typo, known) returns the closest known object by edit distance — e.g. a typo'd 'org.openlore.philosophy.reproducable-builds' returns Some('org.openlore.philosophy.reproducible-builds')
returns None when nothing is within the threshold
the pure helper feeds the CLI empty-result 'Did you mean ...?' line; the CLI then exits 0 (a valid empty result, US-AV-002 Ex 4), NOT a usage error

steps.implementation_notes: Per US-AV-001 + acceptance-tests.md §3 (walking skeleton) + ADR-024/025 + data-models.md §Network index schema + WD-104/WD-121 + I-AV-1 + KPI-AV-3 + DD-AV-13. Q-DELIVER-AV-1: the live index.duckdb migration v1 (the ADR-025 indexed_claims DDL: CID PK, non-Option author_did, verified_against NOT NULL + CHECK <> '', NO UNIQUE on (author,subject,predicate,object), NO consensus/merged table) + the indexed_claims/<did_to_fs_segment>/<cid>.json artifact (slice-03 encoding) land here, driven by AV-1.
